articles.title: APEC部长级圆桌会议在苏州召开，聚焦贸易便利化与供应链韧性
articles.summary: APEC部长级圆桌会议（MRT）于5月22日在苏州开幕，就全球经济不确定性下的供应链韧性与贸易便利化展开讨论。

articles.title: 外交部：中方在APEC推动数字经济与绿色发展双轮驱动
articles.summary: 外交部发言人在例行记者会上表示，中方作为APEC 2026东道主推动数字经济与绿色发展双轮驱动。

articles.summary: APEC economies advance negotiations on the upgraded Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR 2.0) system, aiming to facilitate trusted data flows across the Asia-Pacific region.

articles.summary: APEC members debate competing AI governance models proposed by the US and China, seeking common ground on AI safety standards.

APEC中国年首轮高官会在哈尔滨圆满落幕
articles.summary: 2026年APEC第一次高官会（SOM1）及相关会议在哈尔滨召开，就全年议题框架、数据跨境流动等达成初步共识。

articles.title: Media Registration Opens for Ministers Responsible for Trade Meeting in Suzhou
articles.summary: Media registration has opened for the 2026 APEC Ministers Responsible for Trade Meeting, as preparations advance for a key ministerial platform to address evolving global trade challenges.

articles.title: APEC 2026 priorities: openness, innovation, cooperation under China's host year
articles.summary: China unveils three priority areas for APEC 2026 under the theme 'Building an Asia-Pacific Community for Shared Prosperity': openness, innovation, and cooperation.

articles.title: APEC跨经济体数据隐私规则体系面临新挑战
articles.summary: 专家指出CBPR体系在当前地缘政治环境下参与的成员有限，需要推动更多经济体加入以扩大数据跨境互认范围。

articles.title: East-West Center: APEC digital trade rules need updating for AI era
articles.summary: A new East-West Center policy brief argues APEC's digital trade rules must be updated to address emerging AI cross-border deployment challenges.

articles.title: 中国外交部确认2026 APEC中国年三大优先领域
articles.summary: 外交部发布2026 APEC中国年主题「建设亚太共同体，促进共同繁荣」，宣布三大优先领域：开放、创新、合作。

articles.title: APEC公布2026年会议时间表，中国年内举办超200场会议
articles.summary: APEC秘书处正式发布2026年全年会议日程，中国作为东道主将在哈尔滨、上海、成都、大连、深圳等地举办超过200场会议。

articles.title: CSIS报告：APEC AI治理框架成为美中标准竞争的试金石
articles.summary: CSIS发布报告分析美中两国在APEC框架内围绕人工智能治理标准的竞争态势，认为APEC可能成为协调AI治理规则的重要平台。

articles.title: Applications Open for the 2026 APEC Healthy Women Research Prize
articles.summary: The annual prize encourages the generation of robust women’s health data and research to inform policy and business decisions across APEC member economies.

articles.title: Economies Recast Investment Policy for a Changing Global Landscape
articles.summary: From supply chain resilience to digital investment and sustainability, economies across the Asia-Pacific are using investment policy to recalibrate growth strategies in a more fragmented global economy.

articles.title: Regional Finance Cooperation Advances Household Support, Digital Investment
articles.summary: Deputies endorsed the 2026 work plan for APEC Finance Ministers Process. Outcomes from the meeting will inform discussions ahead of the APEC Finance Ministers’ Meeting, which China plans to host in October in Hong Kong, China.

articles.title: APEC Gains Momentum Amid Rising Risks: Can It Last?
articles.summary: The latest APEC Regional Trends Analysis (ARTA) reveals that growth is expected to remain strong through 2025. However, momentum is set to moderate in 2026 as fiscal conditions tighten and policy uncertainty lingers.

articles.title: APEC Lifts Growth Outlook as Trade Holds, AI Investment Surges
articles.summary: Economic growth in the Asia-Pacific region is projected at 3.2 percent in 2025, with growth expected to hold at 3.1 percent in 2026.

articles.title: ABAC Launches 2026 Work Program Under Theme “Openness, Connectivity, Synergy”
articles.summary: ABAC formally launched its 2026 work program, reaffirming the Asia-Pacific business community’s commitment to open markets, deeper regional integration, and collaborative innovation as foundations for sustainable economic growth.

articles.title: Administrator Liu: Innovation-Led Cooperation Crucial to Reinforce Food Security
articles.summary: Closing the gap between policy commitments and real-world food system resilience is emerging as a priority for APEC economies

articles.title: APEC Health Officials Chart 2026 Health Priorities
articles.summary: Health officials sharpened their focus on health systems as an economic variable.
